trans-unit.source: Attribute Table
trans-unit.target: Attribute Table

trans-unit.source: Each vector layer consists of geometric features (points, lines or polygons) and an __attribute table__ ({numref}`en_vector_data_overview`). The attribute table contains information on each feature in the layer. The information is stored in rows and columns in the attribute table. Each __row__ in the table represents a __feature__, while __columns__ store __attributes__ of that feature. You can use the attribute table to search, sort, filter, edit and select data.
trans-unit.target: Each vector layer consists of geometric features (points, lines or polygons) and an __attribute table__ ({numref}`en_vector_data_overview`). The attribute table contains information on each feature in the layer. The information is stored in rows and columns in the attribute table. Each __row__ in the table represents a __feature__, while __columns__ store __attributes__ of that feature. You can use the attribute table to search, sort, filter, edit and select data.

trans-unit.source: Vector Data overview (Source: HeiGIT). :::
trans-unit.target: Vector Data overview (Source: HeiGIT). :::

trans-unit.source: :::{admonition} Now it's your turn! :class: note
trans-unit.target: :::{admonition} Now it's your turn! :class: note

trans-unit.source: You can complement this chapter by doing the steps with a vector layer of your choice. When working with geodata, you will always have to understand the information stored in the datasets, both the geometries as well as the attribute data.
trans-unit.target: You can complement this chapter by doing the steps with a vector layer of your choice. When working with geodata, you will always have to understand the information stored in the datasets, both the geometries as well as the attribute data.

trans-unit.source: Opening the attribute table
trans-unit.target: Opening the attribute table

trans-unit.source: Having a look into the attribute table is essential to understand and get an overview of the data you are working with. After downloading and importing a dataset into QGIS, you will most likely open the attribute table to understand the data and see what information is available. Understanding what kind of information is available is indispensable when working with GIS software.
trans-unit.target: Having a look into the attribute table is essential to understand and get an overview of the data you are working with. After downloading and importing a dataset into QGIS, you will most likely open the attribute table to understand the data and see what information is available. Understanding what kind of information is available is indispensable when working with GIS software.

trans-unit.source: You can open the attribute table in two ways:
trans-unit.target: You can open the attribute table in two ways:

trans-unit.source: You can also use the shortcut <kbd>F6</kbd> (in some cases <kbd>Fn</kbd> + <kbd>F6</kbd>) to open the attribute.
trans-unit.target: You can also use the shortcut <kbd>F6</kbd> (in some cases <kbd>Fn</kbd> + <kbd>F6</kbd>) to open the attribute.

trans-unit.source: Right click on a layer in the Layers panel and select `Open Attribute Table` ({numref}`en_attributetable_right_click`).
trans-unit.target: Right click on a layer in the Layers panel and select `Open Attribute Table` ({numref}`en_attributetable_right_click`).

trans-unit.source: Opening the attribute table via right-click in QGIS 3.36 :::
trans-unit.target: Opening the attribute table via right-click in QGIS 3.36 :::

trans-unit.source: Select a layer in the Layers panel and click on the attribute table symbol in the toolbar ({numref}`en_attributetable_top_right`).
trans-unit.target: Select a layer in the Layers panel and click on the attribute table symbol in the toolbar ({numref}`en_attributetable_top_right`).

trans-unit.source: If you have multiple layers, only the attribute table of the layer currently selected in the layer panel will open.
trans-unit.target: If you have multiple layers, only the attribute table of the layer currently selected in the layer panel will open.

trans-unit.source: Opening the attribute table in QGIS 3.36 :::
trans-unit.target: Opening the attribute table in QGIS 3.36 :::

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} Buttons of the attribute table :open:
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} Buttons of the attribute table :open:

trans-unit.source: Icon
trans-unit.target: Icon

trans-unit.source: Description
trans-unit.target: Description

trans-unit.source: Purpose
trans-unit.target: Purpose

trans-unit.source: Shortcut
trans-unit.target: Shortcut

trans-unit.source: __Toggle editing mode__
trans-unit.target: __Toggle editing mode__

trans-unit.source: Enable editing functionalities
trans-unit.target: Enable editing functionalities

trans-unit.source: <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>E</kbd>
trans-unit.target: <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>E</kbd>

trans-unit.source: Toggle multi-edit mode
trans-unit.target: Toggle multi-edit mode

trans-unit.source: Update multiple fields of many features
trans-unit.target: Update multiple fields of many features

trans-unit.source: __Save edits__
trans-unit.target: __Save edits__

trans-unit.source: Save current modifications
trans-unit.target: Save current modifications

trans-unit.source: Reload the table
trans-unit.target: Reload the table

trans-unit.source: Add feature
trans-unit.target: Add feature

trans-unit.source: Add new geometry-less feature
trans-unit.target: Add new geometry-less feature

trans-unit.source: Delete selected features
trans-unit.target: Delete selected features

trans-unit.source: Remove selected features from the layer
trans-unit.target: Remove selected features from the layer

trans-unit.source: Cut selected features to clipboard
trans-unit.target: Cut selected features to clipboard

trans-unit.source: <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>X</kbd>
trans-unit.target: <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>X</kbd>

trans-unit.source: Copy selected features to clipboard
trans-unit.target: Copy selected features to clipboard

trans-unit.source: <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>C</kbd>
trans-unit.target: <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>C</kbd>

trans-unit.source: Paste features from clipboard
trans-unit.target: Paste features from clipboard

trans-unit.source: Insert new features from copied ones
trans-unit.target: Insert new features from copied ones

trans-unit.source: <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>V</kbd>
trans-unit.target: <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>V</kbd>

trans-unit.source: Select features using an Expression
trans-unit.target: Select features using an Expression

trans-unit.source: Select All
trans-unit.target: Select All

trans-unit.source: Select all features in the layer
trans-unit.target: Select all features in the layer

trans-unit.source: <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>A</kbd>
trans-unit.target: <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>A</kbd>

trans-unit.source: Invert selection
trans-unit.target: Invert selection

trans-unit.source: Invert the current selection in the layer
trans-unit.target: Invert the current selection in the layer

trans-unit.source: <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>R</kbd>
trans-unit.target: <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>R</kbd>

trans-unit.source: Deselect all
trans-unit.target: Deselect all

trans-unit.source: Deselect all features in the current layer
trans-unit.target: Deselect all features in the current layer

trans-unit.source: <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>Shift</kbd> + <kbd>A</kbd>
trans-unit.target: <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>Shift</kbd> + <kbd>A</kbd>

trans-unit.source: Filter/Select features using form
trans-unit.target: Filter/Select features using form

trans-unit.source: <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>F</kbd>
trans-unit.target: <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>F</kbd>

trans-unit.source: Move selected to top
trans-unit.target: Move selected to top

trans-unit.source: Move selected rows to the top of the table
trans-unit.target: Move selected rows to the top of the table

trans-unit.source: Pan map to the selected rows
trans-unit.target: Pan map to the selected rows

trans-unit.source: <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>P</kbd>
trans-unit.target: <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>P</kbd>

trans-unit.source: Zoom map to the selected rows
trans-unit.target: Zoom map to the selected rows

trans-unit.source: <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>J</kbd>
trans-unit.target: <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>J</kbd>

trans-unit.source: New field
trans-unit.target: New field

trans-unit.source: Add a new field to the data source
trans-unit.target: Add a new field to the data source

trans-unit.source: <Kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>W</kbd>
trans-unit.target: <Kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>W</kbd>

trans-unit.source: Delete field
trans-unit.target: Delete field

trans-unit.source: Remove a field from the data source
trans-unit.target: Remove a field from the data source

trans-unit.source: Organize columns
trans-unit.target: Organize columns

trans-unit.source: Show/hide fields from the attribute table
trans-unit.target: Show/hide fields from the attribute table

trans-unit.source: __Open field calculator__
trans-unit.target: __Open field calculator__

trans-unit.source: Update field for many features in a row
trans-unit.target: Update field for many features in a row

trans-unit.source: <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>I</kbd>
trans-unit.target: <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>I</kbd>

trans-unit.source: Conditional formatting
trans-unit.target: Conditional formatting

trans-unit.source: Enable table formatting
trans-unit.target: Enable table formatting

trans-unit.source: Dock attribute table
trans-unit.target: Dock attribute table

trans-unit.source: Allows to dock or undock the attribute table
trans-unit.target: Allows to dock or undock the attribute table

trans-unit.source: Actions
trans-unit.target: Actions

trans-unit.source: Lists the actions related to the layer
trans-unit.target: Lists the actions related to the layer

trans-unit.source: Sort the attribute table
trans-unit.target: Sort the attribute table

trans-unit.source: You can sort data in the attribute table by clicking on a column header. Text data will be sorted alphabetically and numeric data will be sorted by value. To reverse the sort order, click the header again. A small arrow in the column header indicates whether it is sorted in ascending or descending order.
trans-unit.target: You can sort data in the attribute table by clicking on a column header. Text data will be sorted alphabetically and numeric data will be sorted by value. To reverse the sort order, click the header again. A small arrow in the column header indicates whether it is sorted in ascending or descending order.

trans-unit.source: :::::{grid} 2 ::::{grid-item-card}
trans-unit.target: :::::{grid} 2 ::::{grid-item-card}

trans-unit.source: Attribute table sorted ascendingly. :::
trans-unit.target: Attribute table sorted ascendingly. :::

trans-unit.source: Attribute table sorted descendingly. :::
trans-unit.target: Attribute table sorted descendingly. :::

trans-unit.source: Zoom in on a specific feature via attribute table
trans-unit.target: Zoom in on a specific feature via attribute table

trans-unit.source: You can zoom in on a specific feature if you need to locate it geographically or you want to get a closer look:
trans-unit.target: You can zoom in on a specific feature if you need to locate it geographically or you want to get a closer look:

trans-unit.source: __Zoom:__ Right click on a feature → `Zoom To Feature`.
trans-unit.target: __Zoom:__ Right click on a feature → `Zoom To Feature`.

trans-unit.source: Close your attribute table. The map canvas will now show the selected feature.
trans-unit.target: Close your attribute table. The map canvas will now show the selected feature.

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} Video: Zoom in on a feature
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} Video: Zoom in on a feature

trans-unit.source: Manually select features in the attribute table
trans-unit.target: Manually select features in the attribute table

trans-unit.source: To interact with features in a layer you have to select these features. One way to select features is via the attribute table.
trans-unit.target: To interact with features in a layer you have to select these features. One way to select features is via the attribute table.

trans-unit.source: __Select:__ Click on the lines of the features.
trans-unit.target: __Select:__ Click on the lines of the features.

trans-unit.source: __Multi Select:__ To select multiple features press `Ctrl` and select `features`.
trans-unit.target: __Multi Select:__ To select multiple features press `Ctrl` and select `features`.

trans-unit.source: __Show only selected features:__ In the bottom left of the attribute table open the dropdown menu and select `Show selected features`. To show again all features click on `Show all features`.
trans-unit.target: __Show only selected features:__ In the bottom left of the attribute table open the dropdown menu and select `Show selected features`. To show again all features click on `Show all features`.

trans-unit.source: __Only show unselected features:__ Select features and click on ![](/fig/mActionInvertSelection.png)
trans-unit.target: __Only show unselected features:__ Select features and click on ![](/fig/mActionInvertSelection.png)

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} Video: Manually select features in the attribute table
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} Video: Manually select features in the attribute table

trans-unit.source: Zoom to selected area
trans-unit.target: Zoom to selected area

trans-unit.source: Now that you know how to select features, you can zoom onto your area of interest. To do so you can click on the symbol on the toolbar or right click on the layer and select `Zoom to Selection` ({numref}`en_zoom_to_selection_1`).
trans-unit.target: Now that you know how to select features, you can zoom onto your area of interest. To do so you can click on the symbol on the toolbar or right click on the layer and select `Zoom to Selection` ({numref}`en_zoom_to_selection_1`).

trans-unit.source: Screenshot of how to zoom to Selection on the top. :::
trans-unit.target: Screenshot of how to zoom to Selection on the top. :::

trans-unit.source: Screenshot of how to zoom to Selection by clicking right. :::
trans-unit.target: Screenshot of how to zoom to Selection by clicking right. :::

trans-unit.source: Save only selected features as a new file
trans-unit.target: Save only selected features as a new file

trans-unit.source: After you have selected your data, you might want to proceed with only the selection. You can save your selection as a new layer. To do so right click on the layer → `Export` → `Save only selected features`.
trans-unit.target: After you have selected your data, you might want to proceed with only the selection. You can save your selection as a new layer. To do so right click on the layer → `Export` → `Save only selected features`.

trans-unit.source: Screenshot of how to save only selected features. :::
trans-unit.target: Screenshot of how to save only selected features. :::

trans-unit.source: Now, you can choose the format, layer name and CRS.
trans-unit.target: Now, you can choose the format, layer name and CRS.

trans-unit.source: We recommend using GeoPackage (.gpkg) instead of shapefile (.shp) in most cases. If you are unsure which format is most appropriate, check out the [geodata types](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_geodata_types_wiki.md) page on the wiki.
trans-unit.target: We recommend using GeoPackage (.gpkg) instead of shapefile (.shp) in most cases. If you are unsure which format is most appropriate, check out the [geodata types](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_geodata_types_wiki.md) page on the wiki.

trans-unit.source: Self-Assessment Questions
trans-unit.target: Self-Assessment Questions

trans-unit.source: ::::{admonition} Test your knowledge :class: note
trans-unit.target: ::::{admonition} Test your knowledge :class: note

trans-unit.source: Check whether you know the key concepts from this chapter by answering the questions below.
trans-unit.target: Check whether you know the key concepts from this chapter by answering the questions below.

trans-unit.source: __What is the attribute table in a vector layer, and how is it structured?__
trans-unit.target: __What is the attribute table in a vector layer, and how is it structured?__

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} Answer The attribute table is a tabular representation of the non‑spatial (or descriptive) data for a vector layer. Each row corresponds to one feature (point, line, polygon) in the layer. Each column (also called a field or attribute) stores one attribute/property (e.g. name, population, type) for every feature. :::
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} Answer The attribute table is a tabular representation of the non‑spatial (or descriptive) data for a vector layer. Each row corresponds to one feature (point, line, polygon) in the layer. Each column (also called a field or attribute) stores one attribute/property (e.g. name, population, type) for every feature. :::

trans-unit.source: __How can you open the attribute table in QGIS?__
trans-unit.target: __How can you open the attribute table in QGIS?__

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} Answer
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} Answer

trans-unit.source: In QGIS, right‑click on the layer in the Layers Panel and choose Open Attribute Table.
trans-unit.target: In QGIS, right‑click on the layer in the Layers Panel and choose Open Attribute Table.

trans-unit.source: Alternatively, you can use the Attribute Table button ![](/fig/qgis_open_attribute_table.png) in the toolbar. :::
trans-unit.target: Alternatively, you can use the Attribute Table button ![](/fig/qgis_open_attribute_table.png) in the toolbar. :::

trans-unit.source: __How do you zoom to a specific feature using the attribute table?__
trans-unit.target: __How do you zoom to a specific feature using the attribute table?__

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} Answer
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} Answer

trans-unit.source: Within the attribute table, you can right‑click on a feature’s row (or in a cell) and choose "Zoom to Feature". That changes the map view to focus on that feature. :::
trans-unit.target: Within the attribute table, you can right‑click on a feature’s row (or in a cell) and choose "Zoom to Feature". That changes the map view to focus on that feature. :::

trans-unit.source: __Describe how to manually select features via the attribute table, and how to show only selected or unselected features.__
trans-unit.target: __Describe how to manually select features via the attribute table, and how to show only selected or unselected features.__

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} Answer __Manually selecting features:__
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} Answer __Manually selecting features:__

trans-unit.source: Clicking on a row header of an individual feature in the attribute table selects the feature. It will be highlighted in blue.
trans-unit.target: Clicking on a row header of an individual feature in the attribute table selects the feature. It will be highlighted in blue.

trans-unit.source: To select multiple features, hold <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> or <kbd>Cmd</kbd> on MacOS and click on additional row headers to add more features to the selection.
trans-unit.target: To select multiple features, hold <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> or <kbd>Cmd</kbd> on MacOS and click on additional row headers to add more features to the selection.

trans-unit.source: Holding <kbd>Shift</kbd> let's you select a range of features. __Showing only selected or unselected features:__
trans-unit.target: Holding <kbd>Shift</kbd> let's you select a range of features. __Showing only selected or unselected features:__

trans-unit.source: At the bottom left of the attribute table dialog, there is a drop‑down menu (filter) that lets you switch between:
trans-unit.target: At the bottom left of the attribute table dialog, there is a drop‑down menu (filter) that lets you switch between:

trans-unit.source: __Show All Features__
trans-unit.target: __Show All Features__

trans-unit.source: __Show Selected Features__
trans-unit.target: __Show Selected Features__

trans-unit.source: __Show Unselected Features__
trans-unit.target: __Show Unselected Features__

trans-unit.source: Other filters: Show features visible in map, Show edited and new features, etc.
trans-unit.target: Other filters: Show features visible in map, Show edited and new features, etc.

trans-unit.source: You can also use the ![](/fig/qgis_3.40_move_selection_to_top.png) `Move selected features to the top`-button in the attribute table toolbar. :::
trans-unit.target: You can also use the ![](/fig/qgis_3.40_move_selection_to_top.png) `Move selected features to the top`-button in the attribute table toolbar. :::

trans-unit.source: __Once you have selected a subset of features, how do you save just those features into a new layer (or file)?__
trans-unit.target: __Once you have selected a subset of features, how do you save just those features into a new layer (or file)?__

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} Answer
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} Answer

trans-unit.source: <kbd>Right-click</kbd> on the layer where you selected the features.
trans-unit.target: <kbd>Right-click</kbd> on the layer where you selected the features.

trans-unit.source: Select `Export` → `Save Selected Features as...`. A new window will open.
trans-unit.target: Select `Export` → `Save Selected Features as...`. A new window will open.

trans-unit.source: Choose the data format, specify the save location and file name by clicking on the ![](/fig/Three_points.png)
trans-unit.target: Choose the data format, specify the save location and file name by clicking on the ![](/fig/Three_points.png)

trans-unit.source: Click `Ok`. ::::
trans-unit.target: Click `Ok`. ::::
